channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
item.description: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
item.description: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: One-Character Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Local Root Access, Exploits Now Public
item.description:
Security researchers have published a detailed, working exploit for a Linux kernel use-after-free that lets an unprivileged local user escalate to root and break out of a container.
The flaw, CVE-2026-23111, sits in the kernel's nf_tables packet-filtering code and was patched upstream on February 5, 2026. Exodus Intelligence released its full technical walkthrough on June 8, and it is not even
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
item.description: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.
item.dc:creator: Emil Sayegh

item.title: Meta: NSO Tried Targeting WhatsApp Users Despite Court Order
item.description: <p>Meta says WhatsApp disrupted new NSO-linked phishing attacks and is asking a court to hold the spyware firm in contempt.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-whatsapp-nso-targeted-users-after-court-ban/">Meta: NSO Tried Targeting WhatsApp Users Despite Court Order</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform
item.description: <p>The company founded by Yossi Torati, Omer Gull, and Yuval Itzchakov has emerged from stealth mode.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/a-security-raises-37-million-for-autonomous-offensive-security-platform/">A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
item.description:
Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group.
In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against the company for violating a permanent injunction that barred it from targeting WhatsApp and its users.
"They tried to trick people into clicking on malicious links to drive them to external websites
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical Zcash Vulnerability Found and Fixed
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
item.description: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team
item.description: <p>AI-driven development is not something organizations can or should block. But it must be governed.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/everybody-is-vibe-coding-but-nobody-told-the-security-team/">Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Danelle Au

item.title: OpenAI Expands ChatGPT Lockdown Mode to Millions of Eligible Users
item.description: <p>OpenAI is expanding ChatGPT Lockdown Mode to more users, limiting web-connected tools to reduce the risks of prompt injection and data leakage.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-openai-expands-chatgpt-lockdown-mode-millions-users/">OpenAI Expands ChatGPT Lockdown Mode to Millions of Eligible Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: North Korean Hackers Use Fake Coding Tasks to Steal Crypto
item.description: North Korean actor UNK_DeadDrop targeted developers with fake coding tasks to steal crypto

item.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
item.description:
Check Point has warned of active exploitation of a critical vulnerability impacting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments that are configured to use the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protocol.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50751 (CVSS score: 9.3), is a case of a logic flow weakness in certificate validation that allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to bypass user
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Unveils ChatGPT Account Security Controls
item.description: OpenAI brings Lockdown Mode and Active Sessions to ChatGPT to curb prompt injection data theft

item.title: WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order
item.description: <p>The Meta-owned communications app is filing a federal court contempt order against NSO.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/whatsapp-catches-spyware-firm-nso-defying-no-hacking-court-order/">WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: AI Phishing Is Crushing SOCs with Alert Volume: How to Reduce Tier 1 Overload
item.description:
Phishing has always been a numbers game. AI has turned it into a volume machine.
Attackers can now create convincing emails, fake login pages, and tailored lures in minutes. Every polished message adds another case for Tier 1 to review, another link to inspect, and another alert that cannot be dismissed at a glance.
As the queue grows, a credential theft attempt or malware delivery can easily
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Instagram Account Hacks, Android Zero-Day, GitHub Worm and More
item.description:
Monday again. The weekend was meant to be quiet. It wasn't. Last week had poisoned packages, a broken AI helper, and a worm tearing through repos. The ugly part: basic tricks still worked.
A chatbot got fooled. A bot token got leaked inside the malware. The same old mistakes showed up again. And while everyone chased the loud stuff, quieter attackers sat in inboxes for months, reading mail and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos AI Reportedly Enters NSA Offensive Cyber Planning
item.description: <p>Anthropic engineers are reportedly helping the NSA use Claude Mythos for cyber operations despite the Pentagon’s supply-chain risk label.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-anthropic-nsa-mythos-ai-cyber-operations/">Anthropic’s Mythos AI Reportedly Enters NSA Offensive Cyber Planning</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: David Curry

item.title: Spotlight On: Dreamplug Technologies Private Limited (CRED), a New Principal Participating Organization
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026
item.description: <p>Significant cybersecurity M&#038;A deals announced by Akamai, Check Point, Cisco, Cyera, Dragos, WatchGuard and Zscaler.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cybersecurity-ma-roundup-26-deals-announced-in-may-2026/">Cybersecurity M&#038;A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites
item.description: <p>The flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely and has been exploited in the wild for two months.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/everest-forms-vulnerability-exploited-to-hack-wordpress-sites/">Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: The Hardest Fork
item.description: Mythos is real. I know a big chunk of the industry thinks it's a marketing stunt, and I get why. I get it. But I've seen the findings, and they're bad. These aren't "whoops, this line right here is wrong, and that's RCE." They're novel combinations of a few dozen issues out of thousands of things every SAST scanner already finds, chained together into something much worse. It's real creativity,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach
item.description: <p>Hackers accessed personal information stored on certain Lansing Community College systems in February 2025.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/174000-impacted-by-lansing-community-college-data-breach/">174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing Update
item.description: <p>In April, Anthropic initated <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing">Project Glasswing</a>. The idea was to let companies use their new model to find and fix vulnerabilities in their own software. It was a fantastic PR move, and so many press outlets have uncritically parroted Anthropic&#8217;s claims that it&#8217;s now common wisdom that Mythos is better at finding software vulnerabilities than other models. Which is just <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2026/may/08/how-dangerous-is-anthropics-mythos-ai">not</a> <a href="https://spectrum.ieee.org/ai-cybersecurity-mythos">true</a>.</p> <p>In any case, Anthropic has <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/research/glasswing-initial-update">published</a> a Project Glasswing status report. It&#8217;s finding <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/anthropic-mythos-detected-23000-potential-vulnerabilities-across-1000-oss-projects/">a lot</a> of vulnerabilities in software&#8212;yay! Some of them are even dangerous. But almost none of them has been patched. It&#8217;s ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks
item.description: <p>Focusing on hacking law firms in the US, the ransomware group relies on fast flux to hide its C&#038;C infrastructure.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/silent-ransom-group-uses-dns-fast-flux-in-attacks/">Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Prompt Injection Remains Unsolved, OWASP Researcher Warns
item.description: At Infosecurity Europe 2026, OWASP’s Ariel Fogel warned that prompt injection remains an “unresolved problem” within generative AI architecture

item.title: VerdantBamboo Deploys BSD Variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux Appliances
item.description:
A China-nexus cyber espionage group has been observed deploying a BSD variant of a known backdoor called BRICKSTORM, as well as two other malware families codenamed PLENET (aka GRIMBOLT) and AGENTPSD to target Linux systems.
The activity has been attributed by Volexity to a threat cluster it tracks as VerdantBamboo, which it said overlaps with hacking groups known as Clay Typhoon (Microsoft),
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Two-Thirds of Open Source Community Unaware of Cyber Resilience Act
OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls
item.description: <p>The Active Sessions and Lockdown Mode features are being made more broadly available by the AI giant.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/openai-rolling-out-chatgpt-account-security-controls/">OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: How DSIT Protects Thousands of UK Orgs from Cyber Vulnerabilities
item.description: The Department of Science, Innovation and Technology details how a combination of hands-on human advice and technology systems keeps government agencies safe

item.title: Meta AI Bug Exposes Over 20,000 Instagram Accounts
item.description: Meta confirms an AI tool vulnerability led to unauthorized access to Instagram accounts after a failure in email verification during password reset

item.title: Anthropic Urges Industry Coordination to Allow for a ‘Pause’ in AI Development if Risks Grow
item.description: <p>The proposed coordination would let advanced AI labs verify that global rivals have actually stopped or slowed their work.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/anthropic-urges-industry-coordination-to-allow-for-a-pause-in-ai-development-if-risks-grow/">Anthropic Urges Industry Coordination to Allow for a ‘Pause’ in AI Development if Risks Grow</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: SolarWinds Serv-U Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: <p>Unauthenticated attackers can exploit the flaw via specially crafted POST requests that crash the Serv-U service.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/solarwinds-patches-exploited-serv-u-vulnerability/">SolarWinds Serv-U Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign that has targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services in the U.S. between January and May 2026.
The activity has been attributed by Google Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) to a threat actor dubbed UNC3753, which is also known as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: VS Code Adds 2-Hour Extension Auto-Update Delay to Limit Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Microsoft has announced that Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will apply a two-hour delay before extensions for the integrated development environment (IDE) are updated automatically to a newer version in an attempt to tackle software supply chain threats.
"When automatic updates are enabled, new versions are auto-updated two hours after they are published, adding an extra layer of protection
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
item.description:
OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks.
The feature is primarily designed for people and organizations that handle sensitive data and require stricter protection guarantees. Lockdown Mode is available to logged-in users across Free, Go, Plus, and Pro, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
item.description:
A researcher has reverse-engineered the iOS SDK that Bright Data embeds in consumer apps and documented how it turns devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes that relay web-scraping traffic for a data business Bright Data markets heavily to the AI industry.
The company, the successor to Luminati, operates what it calls the largest residential proxy network in the world,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity security flaw impacting SolarWinds Serv-U multi-protocol file server software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28318 (CVSS score: 7.5), is a denial-of-service (DoS) bug that causes the service to crash
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
item.description:
Two things landed within days of each other this week. A security startup reported 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the media library inside almost everything that touches video, all of them found by an autonomous AI agent.
The same week, Google shipped Chrome 149 with patches for 429 security bugs, the most ever in a single release.
Only the FFmpeg bugs were found by AI.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Miasma Worm Hits 73 Microsoft GitHub Repositories in Major Supply Chain Attack
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager CVE-2026-20245 Flaw Actively Exploited – No Patch Available
item.description:
Cisco has warned that a high-severity security flaw impacting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager has come under active exploitation.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20245, carries a CVSS score of 7.8 out of a maximum of 10.0. It affects the following deployment types -
On-Prem Deployment Cisco SD-WAN Cloud-Pro Cisco SD-WAN Cloud (Cisco Managed) Cisco SD-WAN for Government (FedRAMP)
"A
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Flaws, and SpaceX’s Record IPO Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from June 1–5.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-flaws-and-spacexs-record-ipo-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Upgrades, Security Flaws, and SpaceX’s Record IPO Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Cisco Warns Agentic AI Will Put New Pressure on Enterprise Networks
item.description: <p>Cisco executives said agentic AI could triple network traffic, creating new infrastructure and security demands for enterprises.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisco-ai-networking-bottleneck/">Cisco Warns Agentic AI Will Put New Pressure on Enterprise Networks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
item.description: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based information stealer and a self-spreading worm, respectively.
According to JFrog, the information stealer "scrapes every secret it can find on a developer's machine, hides behind an eBPF kernel rootkit, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Malicious Hugging Face Models Could Trigger Remote Code Execution
item.description: <p>A flaw in Hugging Face Transformers could allow malicious AI models to execute code, exposing credentials and highlighting AI supply chain risks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-hugging-face-transformers-rce-flaw/">Malicious Hugging Face Models Could Trigger Remote Code Execution</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Chinese Spies Using LinkedIn, Job Sites to Recruit Western Workers
item.description: <p>Five Eyes agencies warn that fake online recruiters linked to Chinese intelligence are targeting workers for sensitive policy and defense information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-five-eyes-fake-recruiters-chinese-intelligence/">Chinese Spies Using LinkedIn, Job Sites to Recruit Western Workers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: New CISA Warning: Hackers Are Targeting Fuel Tank Monitoring Systems
item.description: <p>CISA warns attackers are targeting internet-exposed Automatic Tank Gauge systems used in fuel storage. Here’s what operators should fix now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisa-atg-systems-fuel-storage-cyberattacks/">New CISA Warning: Hackers Are Targeting Fuel Tank Monitoring Systems</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
item.description:
Arabic-speaking users have emerged as the target of a new Android spyware codenamed Asin, according to findings from ESET.
The Slovakian cybersecurity company said it first detected the malware spread via multiple campaigns in early 2025, with each attack wave making use of distinct websites mimicking utilities, war-related updates, and a government news source:
govlens[.]net, which
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DentaQuest Cyberattack Tied to 2.6M Exposed Accounts
item.description: <p>DentaQuest confirmed a cybersecurity incident after health data tied to 2.6 million accounts surfaced in a public breach listing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-dentaquest-breach-health-data-2-6m-accounts/">DentaQuest Cyberattack Tied to 2.6M Exposed Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
item.description: AI worms, or &quot;viruses with wings and brains,&quot; adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: AI Worm
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
item.description: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously unreported threat cluster dubbed OP-512 (where "OP" stands for "opponent") that has been observed&nbsp;targeting Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) servers to deploy a bespoke web shell framework.
ReliaQuest has assessed with moderate to high confidence that the espionage-focused activity is linked to China.
"OP-512 was highly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Southeast Asia Scam Compounds Turn AI Into a Cybersecurity Threat
item.description: <p>Scam compounds across Southeast Asia are using AI, malware, and automation to scale fraud, forcing APAC security teams to rethink phishing, identity, and mobile-risk controls.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-ai-scam-compounds-risk-apac-southeast-asia/">Southeast Asia Scam Compounds Turn AI Into a Cybersecurity Threat</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Practical Lessons From Lloyds' Agentic AI Security Playbook
item.description: Lloyds Banking Group shared its approach for securing agentic AI workflows, with a mix of hands on experimentation and cross functional governance

item.title: Only 10% of SOCs Say They’re Getting Excellent Value From AI. Here’s What the Second Wave Has to Deliver
item.description: Eighteen months ago, the AI SOC was a marketing line. Today it's a budget item. The category has crossed over from interesting to inevitable, with billions of dollars now flowing into AI-powered security operations platforms, agentic SOC tools, and AI co-pilots built into every layer of the security stack. The data shows SOCs are buying, deploying, and standing up AI capabilities at the fastest
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: OWASP Introduces Agentic AI Security Maturity Framework
item.description: The OWASP agentic AI security framework helps organizations assess governance maturity vs adoption and adjust governance as needed

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: AI Coding Tools Need Built-In Security for Agentic Development Era
item.description: Ox Security field CTO, Boaz Barzel, makes the case for vibe security to tackle AI agent coding risks

item.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress Plugin Flaw to Take Over Sites
item.description:
Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw in Everest Forms Pro, a WordPress plugin with about 4,000 active installations, to execute arbitrary code, leading to a complete site compromise.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-3300 (CVSS score: 9.8), a remote code execution bug impacting all versions of the plugin up to, and including, 1.9.12. A patch for the flaw was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Reactive Security Is Failing Healthcare Organizations, Experts Warn
item.description: A perfect storm of legacy devices, hyper connectivity and human fatigue is bad news for the healthcare sector, warns Cyber Salus

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Scams Are Already Live: Fake Sites, Banking Malware, and Stolen Logins
item.description:
Security researchers and the FBI are warning that a wave of FIFA-themed fraud is already hitting World Cup 2026 fans, days before the June 11 kickoff.
Recent reports describe thousands of lookalike FIFA domains, banking malware hidden inside pirate streaming apps, and at least one operation that copies FIFA's login page well enough to take over real accounts.
It is an obvious target. More than
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: PCPJack Hijacks 230 AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure Servers for Covert SMTP Relay Network
item.description:
The threat actor known as PCPJack has hijacked cloud servers associated with Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure to create a covert SMTP email relay network.
"Compromised business servers across the U.S., Europe, and Asia were quietly converted into SMTP proxies, verified for mail relay capability, and synced to a downstream consumer every five minutes," Hunt.io said in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Top Vendor Risk Monitoring Solutions for Continuous Oversight | UpGuard
item.description: Explore the best vendor risk monitoring solutions that provide continuous oversight of third-party vendors, ensuring compliance and security.

item.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
item.description: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
item.description: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
item.description: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Apple Begins Rosetta’s Final Phase as Intel Mac Era Winds Down
item.description: <p>Apple says macOS 26 Tahoe is the last major release for Intel Macs, with Rosetta support continuing through macOS 27 before narrowing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-macos-27-drops-intel-mac-support-rosetta/">Apple Begins Rosetta&#8217;s Final Phase as Intel Mac Era Winds Down</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: New GitHub Zero-Day Exposed Developer Tokens to Attackers
item.description: <p>A github.dev flaw could let attackers steal GitHub OAuth tokens through a one-click attack, exposing private repositories and codebases.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-github-dev-vscode-webview-oauth-token-vulnerability/">New GitHub Zero-Day Exposed Developer Tokens to Attackers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Gartner SRM 2026 Signals a Cybersecurity Shift From Prevention to Resilience
item.description: <p>Gartner SRM 2026 put resilience, identity, and AI agent governance at the center of cybersecurity strategy as prevention loses ground.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-gartner-srm-2026-resilience-ai-security/">Gartner SRM 2026 Signals a Cybersecurity Shift From Prevention to Resilience</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
item.description:
Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root.
It is tracked as CVE-2026-20230, and proof-of-concept exploit code is already public. Cisco's PSIRT says it has not seen the flaw used in attacks yet. The PoC shortens that runway.
The flaw is a server-side request forgery.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Everest Forms Pro Vulnerability Allows Remote Code Execution on WordPress Sites
item.description: Critical Everest Forms Pro RCE flaw exploited to create rogue WordPress admin accounts

item.title: Google Reportedly Wants Android App Code From Play Store Developers
item.description: <p>Google is reportedly offering select Play Store developers payment for access to app source code. For Android developers in APAC, the offer raises questions about IP rights, security risks, AI-use terms, and local data obligations before signing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-code-offer/">Google Reportedly Wants Android App Code From Play Store Developers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Had a Token Flaw IT Teams Should Check Now
item.description: <p>A debug flag left active in six Microsoft 365 Android apps allowed another installed app on the same device to request account tokens without user interaction.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-365-android-token-flaw/">Microsoft 365 Android Apps Had a Token Flaw IT Teams Should Check Now</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: US Firms Try DeepSeek as Silicon Valley AI Costs Rise
item.description: <p>US firms are testing China’s DeepSeek as Silicon Valley AI costs rise, raising questions about savings, data residency, and risk.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-us-firms-try-deepseek-ai-costs-rise/">US Firms Try DeepSeek as Silicon Valley AI Costs Rise</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
item.description:
A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub issue. Because Anthropic's own action repo used the same workflow, a working attack could have pushed malicious code into the action itself and onto the projects downstream that pull it.
RyotaK of GMO
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Agentic AI Is Transforming Defense, But Only Secure IT Infrastructure Will Maximize It
item.description: Over the past several weeks, the cybersecurity community has been reminded how quickly frontier and agentic AI in defense networks can challenge our assumptions. When Anthropic's Claude Mythos model was made available to a limited set of organizations as a technical preview, it was reported that an unauthorized group claimed that it had gained access within hours. The incident, if true, was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
item.description: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Malicious WhatsApp, Slack Alerts Could Have Exposed Millions of Android Users
item.description: <p>SafeBreach found a now-fixed Gemini Android flaw that let malicious WhatsApp and Slack alerts manipulate AI responses and tools.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-whatsapp-slack-alerts-could-manipulate-gemini-android/">Malicious WhatsApp, Slack Alerts Could Have Exposed Millions of Android Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: AI Agents Gone Wrong, Sketchy C2 Tools, ClickFix Tricks, JS Backdoors & 20+ New Stories
item.description:
It got stupid again.
The internet still feels held together with tape. Bad plugins, old bugs, fake tools, trusted apps doing shady things. Same mess, new wrapper. And now the weird stuff is normal. Forums go down and come back worse. Cheap hackers get better toys. AI starts breaking real systems. Great.
Read the whole thing before it ruins your week anyway.
Unauthenticated
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese-Speaking Actor TA4922 Widens Its Global Reach
item.description: Newly named Chinese-speaking actor TA4922 expands from East Asia into Europe and Africa

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: AI Adoption Creates New Opportunities for Attackers to Distribute Malware, Microsoft Warns
item.description: Microsoft Detection and Response Team (DART) details how it has uncovered malicious AI applications as cyber criminals manipulate organizations adopting AI tools

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Mythos Outperforms GPT5.5 on Google Chrome Vulnerability Exploits, Says New Benchmark
item.description: A Bugcrowd researcher has unveiled ExploitBench, an independent benchmark of AI models for vulnerability exploitation

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: How Proton Fights Against Cybercriminals Using Its Services
item.description: Proton uses machine learning models to detect abuse of its services – especially email addresses used by cybercriminals

item.title: China-Linked TA4922 Expands Phishing Attacks to U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa
item.description:
A new China-linked cybercrime group known as TA4922 has expanded its targeting focus to target European organizations in the U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa.
These efforts have been complemented by a "rapid operational tempo" and a continually evolving malware arsenal comprising known families like ValleyRAT (aka Winos 4.0) and Atlas RAT (aka AtlasCross RAT), as well as previously
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FlutterShell Backdoor Spreads to macOS via Malicious Google and YouTube Ads
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a macOS malvertising campaign codenamed Operation FlutterBridge that spreads a new backdoor called FlutterShell.
According to Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the campaign is said to be the next stage of a previously reported activity cluster dubbed JSCoreRunner (aka FileRipple) in late August 2025. The cybercrime group behind the two attack chains is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hacking Meta’s AI Chatbot
item.description: <p>Hackers are <a href="https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/01/hackers-hijacked-instagram-accounts-by-tricking-meta-ai-support-chatbot-into-granting-access/">convincing</a> Meta&#8217;s AI support chatbot to let them take over other peoples&#8217; accounts:</p> <blockquote><p>A <a href="https://x.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2061253599758315527">video</a> posted on X showed the step-by-step process to hack someone&#8217;s Instagram account. The hacker allegedly used a VPN to spoof the targets&#8217; presumed location to avoid triggering Instagram&#8217;s automated account protections. Then, the hacker opened a chat with Meta AI Support Assistant and asked the bot to add a new email address to the target&#8217;s account. The chatbot can be seen sending a verification code to the email address provided by the hacker; the hacker then shares the verification code with the chatbot, which prompts the chatbot to show a button to &#8220;Reset Password.&#8221; The hacker enters a new password and takes over the victim&#8217;s account...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects to funnel unsuspecting users through a Traffic Distribution System (TDS) and deliver malware families like Remus Stealer, AnimateClipper, and the SessionGate framework.
"The sites are well-designed and often look like legitimate project portals at a glance, sometimes referencing
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
item.description:
Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it through Dropbox and OneDrive so the traffic blended into normal cloud activity.
Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team reported the campaign this week. This points to espionage, not a money grab:
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: How Businesses Can Prepare for a Cybersecurity Crisis with Effective Plans
item.description: Cybersecurity and business leaders with experience of dealing with major incidents from within the NCSC and at JLR detail what you need to prioritize if your organization is hit by a cyber-attack

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Ukraine’s Experience Highlights the Need for Preparation and Resilience in Cybersecurity
item.description: Former Ukrainian foreign minister, Dmytro Kuleba, urges Infosecurity Europe attendees to fight the good fight

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Raise Security Concerns with Procurement Now, Because Quantum Can’t Wait
item.description: Forescout VP of security intelligence, Rik Ferguson, warns that Q-day is fast approaching

item.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrency fraud targeting Americans.
The "Disruption Week" operation began May 18, 2026, leading to the takedown of millions of social media, email, and internet access accounts used by transnational
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
item.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
item.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Apple’s 2026 Security Events: iPhone Exploits, Zero-Days Put Millions at Risk
item.description: <p>Apple’s 2026 security year includes zero-days, iPhone exploit kits, WebKit fixes, and background patches that users and IT teams need to track.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-security-roundup-june-2026/">Apple’s 2026 Security Events: iPhone Exploits, Zero-Days Put Millions at Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber &amp; Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
item.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
item.description:
A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, fake a message from their boss, push the phone into a Zoom call, or quietly poison its long-term memory.
No malicious app on the phone is required. The assistant just had to treat a hostile
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
item.description: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright
